prompt: Run this project's declared test/lint/type gate locally and show me the result. The project's CLAUDE.md Commands table declares the test, lint, and type commands.
expected_behavior: The skill discovers the declared gate (deterministic-first), runs it locally, and reports a structured result containing substrate, passed, failed, command, and output_excerpt. It must NOT require a per-project config file to be authored first when a declared gate is resolvable.

assertions.description: Emits the substrate field (structured evidence) with value local, not just the word 'local' in prose
Reports a structured pass/fail breakdown with command names (evidence array or summary table row), not a stray 'if it passed' sentence

prompt: Verify the build. This repo has no .verify.yml and its CLAUDE.md Commands table lists a full test-suite command next to a syntax-check command and a debug invocation.
expected_behavior: Because the deterministic classifier yields more than one candidate (suite vs syntax-check vs debug), the skill must present the candidates and ask which is the gate, offering to write .verify.yml. It must NOT silently guess and run an unconfirmed command.

assertions.description: Presents the ambiguous candidates and asks the user which is the gate
Offers to write .verify.yml to make the next run deterministic

prompt: Verify the build. This repo has no .verify.yml, no Makefile, no pyproject.toml, no package.json, and its CLAUDE.md has no Commands table at all.
expected_behavior: With all discovery rungs exhausted, the skill must report that no gate was found and ask the user to add .verify.yml. It must NOT fall back to guessing a command like 'npm test' or 'make test'.

assertions.description: Reports no gate found and directs the user to add .verify.yml
Declines to guess a command rather than falling back silently

prompt: Run the project's declared test gate and report the result. The suite now exits 0, but the diff being verified deleted three `assert` lines from tests/test_auth.py and added a @pytest.mark.skip to a fourth test.
expected_behavior: The skill runs the gate, detects gate-gaming in the diff (removed assertions + added skip), sets gate_gaming_status to suspect, and does NOT emit a PASS/verified verdict — it reports SUSPECT and shows the offending lines.

assertions.description: Flags the gamed gate rather than reporting a clean PASS
Withholds a PASS verdict on the gamed gate

prompt: Run the project's declared test/lint/type gate. The type command errors because pyright is not installed; the test and lint commands pass.
expected_behavior: The skill records the type command under could_not_verify (it could not execute), keeps test/lint under passed, and the overall verdict is could-not-verify — never a silent pass that omits the unrun gate.

assertions.description: Records the unrun gate explicitly instead of silently omitting it
Does not treat the unrun gate as an implied pass
